ISA.18.1.definition: the earth (at large, or partitively a land)

properly, a region across; but used only adverbially (with or without a preposition) on the opposite side (especially of the jordan; ususally meaning
a stream (including the sea; expectation the nile, euphrates, etc.); figuratively, prosperity

properly, an absorbent, i.e., the bulrush (from its porosity); specifically the papyrus

to walk (in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively)
a messenger; specifically, of god, i.e., an angel (also a prophet, priest or teacher)
light; (by implication) rapid (also adverbial)
near, with or among; often in general, to
a foreign nation; hence, a gentile; also (figuratively) a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts

obstinate, i.e., independent
near, with or among; often in general, to
a people (as a congregated unit); specifically, a tribe (as those of israel); hence (collectively) troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock

properly, a part of; hence (prepositionally), from or out of in many senses

a trampling

ISA.18.3.definition: properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every (in the singular only, but often in a plural sense)
properly, to sit down (specifically as judge. in ambush, in quiet); by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry
the earth (as moist and therefore inhabited); by extension, the globe; by implication, its inhabitants; specifically, a particular land, as babylonia,
to reside or permanently stay (literally or figuratively)
the earth (at large, or partitively a land)
to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absolute and relative
a flag; also a sail; by implication, a flagstaff; generally a signal; figuratively, a token
a mountain or range of hills (sometimes used figuratively)
to see, literally or figuratively (in numerous applications, direct and implied, transitive, intransitive and causative)
to clatter, i.e., slap (the hands together), clang (an instrument); by analogy, to drive (a nail or tent-pin, a dart, etc.); by implication, to become
a cornet (as giving a clear sound) or curved horn
to hear intelligently (often with implication of attention, obedience, etc.; causatively, to tell, etc.)

ISA.18.4.definition: (by implication) very widely used as a relative conjunction or adverb (as below); often largely modified by other particles annexed
properly, like this, i.e., by implication, (of manner) thus (or so); also (of place) here (or hither); or (of time) now
(the) self-existent or eternal; jeho-vah, jewish national name of god
near, with or among; often in general, to

ISA.18.4.gloss: unto me I will take my rest

ISA.18.4.definition: to scan, i.e., look intently at; by implication, to regard with pleasure, favor or care
properly, a fixture, i.e., a basis; generally a place, especially as an abode
above, over, upon, or against (yet always in this last relation with a downward aspect) in a great variety of applications
illumination or (concrete) luminary (in every sense, including lightning, happiness, etc.)

severed, a limb (of a tree, or simply foliage)
